title: Cash Budget with Borrowing
event: Reyes Co. begins July with $20,000 cash, expects receipts of $190,000 and disbursements of $185,000, and must maintain a $30,000 minimum cash balance. Compute the required borrowing.

computationPaths.narration: Run the cash to its preliminary ending balance, then compare against the floor — the shortfall is the loan.

memorize.body: Preliminary balance = beginning + receipts − disbursements. Borrow enough to reach the minimum; repay when above it.
A positive preliminary balance can still force borrowing — the test is against the MINIMUM, not against zero.

questions.prompt: Preliminary cash balance $25,000, required minimum $30,000. Borrowing needed = ?

distractors.feedback: Positive isn't enough — the floor is 30,000. Five thousand short means a 5,000 loan.
That's the minimum itself. Borrow only the GAP between the floor and the 25,000 on hand.
